author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
Ambrose Most Highly Praised by Pelagius.
title: Ambrose Most Highly Praised by Pelagius.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Chapter 47 [XLIII.]—Ambrose Most Highly Praised by Pelagius.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: “The blessed Bishop Ambrose,” says he, “in whose writings the Roman faith shines forth with especial brightness, and whom the Latins have always regarded as the very flower and glory of their authors, and who has never found a foe bold enough to censure his faith or the purity of his understanding of the Scriptures.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: Observe the sort as well as the amount of the praises which he bestows; nevertheless, however holy and learned he is, he is not to be compared to the authority of the canonical Scripture.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: The reason of this high commendation of Ambrose lies in the circumstance, that Pelagius sees proper to quote a certain passage from his writings to prove that man is able to live without sin.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: This, however, is not the question before us.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Grace of Christ, and on Original Sin.
verses.text: We are at present discussing that assistance of grace which helps us towards avoiding sin, and leading holy lives.
